hero.title: Admissions
hero.subtitle: Begin your journey toward excellence at Angeles University Foundation. Explore testing schedules, application steps, programs, and scholarships to find your path.

testing.title: Testing Dates
testing.description: AUF administers admission tests to assess applicants' academic readiness. Review the examination schedules, registration requirements, and test guidelines below.
testing.note: Test schedules are updated each academic term. For the most current schedule, contact the Testing and Admissions Office at admissions@auf.edu.ph.

examTypes.name: AUF College Admission Test
examTypes.description: The primary admission examination for all incoming freshmen applicants to undergraduate programs. Covers verbal ability, numerical reasoning, abstract reasoning, and general knowledge.
examTypes.eligibility: Senior High School graduates or incoming Grade 12 students within the academic year of application.
registrationSteps: Accomplish and submit the online application form at admissions.auf.edu.ph
Upload required documents (Form 138, Certificate of Good Moral Character, 2×2 ID photo)
Receive examination schedule and permit via email

examTypes.name: Transfer Admission Test
examTypes.description: For applicants with at least one year of college credits from another institution. Includes a subject-area component relevant to the degree program being applied for.
examTypes.eligibility: College-level transferees with Official Transcript of Records (OTR) from previous school.
registrationSteps: Obtain a Transfer Credential from the previous school
Submit transfer application form with OTR and honorable dismissal

examTypes.name: Graduate School Admission Test
examTypes.description: Required for all applicants to master's and doctoral programs. Assesses critical thinking, research aptitude, and discipline-specific foundational knowledge.
examTypes.eligibility: Bachelor's degree holders (or equivalent) applying to graduate programs. Some programs may require additional credentials.
registrationSteps: Submit Graduate School application form with academic credentials
Provide two letters of recommendation from former professors or supervisors
Complete personal statement/letter of intent as required by the program

schedule.registrationDeadline: At least 1 week before preferred exam date

applicationGuide.title: Application Guide
applicationGuide.intro: Applying to AUF is a straightforward process. Follow these steps to complete your application from start to enrollment.

steps.title: Choose Your Program
steps.description: Explore AUF's colleges and programs to find the right fit for your interests and career goals. Browse the Academics section or visit college microsites for detailed program information.
tips: Review admission requirements specific to your chosen program
Consider attending an open house or campus tour
Talk to current students or alumni of the program

steps.title: Accomplish the Online Application
steps.description: Complete the AUF online application form at admissions.auf.edu.ph. Provide accurate and complete personal, academic, and contact information.
tips: Prepare a valid email address — all communications will be sent there
Note your application reference number for follow-up

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Upload or submit the following documents to the Admissions Office. Incomplete submissions may delay your application.
requirements: Certificate of Good Moral Character (signed by the school principal)
School ID or any valid government ID
For transferees: Official Transcript of Records and Honorable Dismissal

steps.title: Pay the Application and Testing Fee
steps.description: Settle the non-refundable application and testing fee at the AUF Cashier or via the designated online payment channels. Present proof of payment to the Admissions Office.
tips: Keep your official receipt — you will need it to claim your test permit
Online payment options available at admissions.auf.edu.ph

steps.title: Take the AUF Admission Test
steps.description: Appear at the designated venue on your scheduled examination date. Bring your test permit and a valid ID. Arrive at least 30 minutes before the exam.
tips: No entrance will be allowed after the exam has started
Calculators and electronic devices are not permitted unless specified
Dress appropriately and follow campus policies

steps.title: Await Examination Results
steps.description: Results are typically released within a week of the examination date. You will receive an email notification at the address provided in your application. You may also check the Admissions Office in person.
tips: Check your spam/junk folder if you do not receive an email
Results are valid for one academic year only

steps.title: Complete Enrollment
steps.description: Upon receiving your Notice of Acceptance, proceed to enrollment during the designated enrollment period. Bring all original documents and the required enrollment fees.
tips: Enrollment periods are strictly observed — do not miss your assigned schedule
Secure your slot early as programs have limited enrollment capacity
Consult the Admissions Office for scholarship and financial assistance applications

programs.title: Academic Programs
programs.description: AUF offers a wide range of undergraduate, graduate, and professional degree programs across 12 colleges and schools. Click on any college to visit its microsite and explore detailed program information.
programs.note: For specific admission requirements per program, contact the relevant college dean's office or the Admissions Office at admissions@auf.edu.ph.

calendar.title: Academic Calendar
calendar.description: The AUF Academic Calendar is divided into two regular semesters and a summer term. Key dates are aligned with the CHED-prescribed academic calendar.
calendar.note: Specific dates are confirmed each academic year. The official calendar is published by the Office of the University Registrar at the start of each academic year. Contact registrar@auf.edu.ph for the latest schedule.

events.notes: By year level and college schedule
By year level and college schedule

scholarships.title: Scholarships and Grants
scholarships.description: AUF is committed to making quality education accessible. A range of scholarships, grants-in-aid, and financial assistance programs are available to qualified students.
scholarships.contactNote: For scholarship inquiries and application requirements, contact the Student Affairs Office at sao@auf.edu.ph or the Finance Office at finance@auf.edu.ph.

categories.name: Academic Excellence Scholarships
categories.description: Awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding academic performance. Recipients are identified based on their academic standing at the end of each semester.
benefits: Tuition fee discount or full scholarship depending on academic standing
Renewable each semester upon maintaining the required GPA
Recognition in the Dean's List and President's List
eligibility: Must be a full-time student with at least 18 academic units
Must maintain a specified minimum GPA (varies per scholarship tier)
Must have no failing grades or incomplete marks

categories.name: Athletic Scholarship
categories.description: Granted to student-athletes who represent AUF in official intercollegiate competitions. Recognizes the dual commitment to academic excellence and athletic achievement.
benefits: Partial to full tuition coverage depending on athletic performance and program
Allowance for training and competition equipment
eligibility: Must be a member of an official AUF varsity team
Must maintain a minimum academic standing as required by the Sports Office
Recommended by the Sports and Athletics Office

categories.name: Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES)
categories.description: A CHED-administered government subsidy under the Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act (Republic Act 10931). Provides direct financial assistance to enrolled students in private HEIs.
benefits: Annual subsidy credited directly to tuition and fees
Available to eligible students enrolled in recognized private colleges
eligibility: Must be a Filipino citizen enrolled in an accredited program
Must belong to an eligible household based on DSWD assessment
Must not be a recipient of any full scholarship that covers all tuition costs

categories.name: ESGP-PA (Expanded Students' Grants-in-Aid Program for Poverty Alleviation)
categories.description: Provides financial assistance to students from the bottom 20% of Philippine households, enabling them to access quality higher education.
benefits: Tuition and other school fees coverage
eligibility: Must be from a household beneficiary of the Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps)
Must pass the AUF admission examination

categories.name: External and Corporate Scholarships
categories.description: Various scholarships funded by corporations, foundations, and government agencies partnered with AUF. Available to students in specific disciplines.
benefits: Tuition coverage and stipend (varies per sponsor)
Employment preference after graduation (for select corporate scholarships)
eligibility: Eligibility criteria vary per scholarship program and sponsor
Contact the Student Affairs Office for the current list of available corporate scholarships

registrar.title: University Registrar
registrar.description: The Office of the University Registrar (OUR) is the official custodian of all academic records at AUF. It provides a range of certification and records services to current students, graduates, and other authorized parties.

services.name: Official Transcript of Records (TOR)
services.description: Official document certifying a student's academic history at AUF, signed and sealed by the Registrar. Required for employment, graduate school applications, board exams, and professional licensure.

services.name: Certificate of Registration (COR)
services.description: Official proof of enrollment for the current semester. Required for government transactions, scholarship applications, and other purposes.
services.processingTime: Issued during enrollment period; requests for re-issuance within 1 working day

services.name: Certificate of Graduation / Completion
services.description: Official certification that a student has completed all academic requirements for their degree program.

services.name: Document Authentication
services.description: Authentication of AUF-issued academic documents for use in government agencies, foreign institutions, or apostille purposes.

services.name: Certificate of Good Moral Character
services.description: Official certification attesting to a student's good standing and conduct at AUF. Required for scholarship applications, transferees, and employment.

services.name: Enrollment Verification
services.description: Verification letter confirming a student's current enrollment status, year level, and program. Required for insurance claims, government benefits, and certain employment applications.

downloadableForms.name: Records Request Form
downloadableForms.description: General form for TOR, certifications, and authentication requests

downloadableForms.name: Authorization Letter Template
downloadableForms.description: For proxy claims of official documents

downloadableForms.name: Transfer Request Form
downloadableForms.description: For students requesting transfer to another institution

downloadableForms.name: Cross-Enrollment Form
downloadableForms.description: For students enrolled at AUF who wish to take subjects at another institution

downloadableForms.name: Leave of Absence Form
downloadableForms.description: For students applying for temporary leave from studies
